    "MUST be present": "The ClientID MUST be present and is the first field in the CONNECT packet Payload [MQTT-3.1.3-3]",
    "MUST be a UTF-8 Encoded String": "The ClientID MUST be a UTF-8 Encoded String as defined in section 1.5.4 [MQTT-3.1.3-4]",
    "MUST allow ClientIDs which are between 1 and 23 UTF-8 encoded bytes in length": "The Server MUST allow ClientIDs which are between 1 and 23 UTF-8 encoded bytes in length, and that contain only the characters \"0123456789abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ\" [MQTT-3.1.3-5]",
    "MUST treat the zero-byte ClientID as a special case and assign a unique ClientID to that Client": "however if the Server does so the Server MUST treat the zero-byte ClientID as a special case and assign a unique ClientID to that Client [MQTT-3.1.3-6]",
    "MUST then process the CONNECT packet as if the Client had provided that unique ClientID": "The Server MUST then process the CONNECT packet as if the Client had provided that unique ClientID, and MUST return the Assigned Client Identifier in the CONNACK packet [MQTT-3.1.3-7]",
    "MUST return the Assigned Client Identifier in the CONNACK packet": "and MUST return the Assigned Client Identifier in the CONNACK packet [MQTT-3.1.3-7]",
    "MUST close the Network Connection": "and then the Server MUST close the Network Connection [MQTT-3.1.3-8]"
}

MUST be closed whether or not the CONNACK or DISCONNECT is sent
cannot: cannot be anticipated by the sender

MUST NOT: The Server MUST NOT match Topic Filters starting with a wildcard character (# or +) with Topic Names beginning with a character [MQTT-4.7.2-1]
SHOULD: The Server SHOULD prevent Clients from using such Topic Names to exchange messages with other Clients
cannot: Applications cannot use a topic with a leading character for their own purposes
A subscription to # will not receive any messages published to a topic beginning with a leading character
A subscription to +/monitor/Clients will not receive any messages published to SYS/monitor/Clients
For a Client to receive messages from topics that begin with SYS/ and from topics that do not begin with a leading character, it has to subscribe to both # and SYS/#

Mandatory: MUST set the Authentication Method to the same value as the Authentication Method originally used to authenticate the Network Connection [MQTT-4.12.1-1]
MUST close the Network Connection [MQTT-4.12.1-2]
Recommended: SHOULD send DISCONNECT with an appropriate Reason Code as described in section 4.13

MUST: MUST be at least one character long
MUST NOT include the null character (Unicode U+0000)
MUST NOT encode to more than 65,535 bytes
MUST NOT perform any normalization of Topic Names or Topic Filters, or any modification or substitution of unrecognized characters
MUST NOT: MUST NOT include the null character (Unicode U+0000)
MUST NOT encode to more than 65,535 bytes
MUST NOT perform any normalization of Topic Names or Topic Filters, or any modification or substitution of unrecognized characters

should check that the Client is authorized to use the Client Identifier

should consider limiting access

Mandatory: MUST use one of the DISCONNECT Reason Code values
Prohibited: cannot be processed by this implementation
is not accepted by this Server
is not accepted by this Client or Server
is not authorized
is not supported
is not accepted

Mandatory: It is the responsibility of the Requester to make sure the Requester has the necessary authority to publish to the request topic, and to subscribe to the Topic Name that the Requester sets in the Response Topic property.
It is the responsibility of the Responder to make sure the Responder has the authority to subscribe to the request topic and publish to the Response Topic.
Recommended: it is recommended that Servers implement topic authorization.
It is strongly recommended that Server implementations that offer TLS should use TCP port 8883

5 Security (non-normative) -> 5.4 Implem.data:
{
    "should be aware of": "SHOULD",
    "might allow for": "MAY",
    "can be used": "MAY",
    "requires support for": "MUST",
    "can provide": "MAY"
}

Mandatory: choose to validate UTF-8 Encoded strings
checking that the Disallowed Unicode code points are not used
Recommended: Validating UTF-8 Encoded strings avoids implementation difficulties
also protects applications from having to process them
Validating that these code points are not used removes some security exposures

should: should have a transitive trust relationship

MUST: MUST assign an unused Packet Identifier when the sender has a new Application Message to publish
MUST send a PUBLISH packet containing this Packet Identifier with QoS 2 and DUP flag set to 0
MUST treat the PUBLISH packet as unacknowledged until the sender has received the corresponding PUBREC packet from the receiver
MUST send a PUBREL packet when the sender receives a PUBREC packet from the receiver with a Reason Code value less than 0x80
This PUBREL packet MUST contain the same Packet Identifier as the original PUBLISH packet
MUST treat the PUBREL packet as unacknowledged until the sender has received the corresponding PUBCOMP packet from the receiver
MUST respond with a PUBREC containing the Packet Identifier from the incoming PUBLISH packet, having accepted ownership of the Application Message
the receiver MUST treat any subsequent PUBLISH packet that contains that Packet Identifier as being a new Application Message
the receiver MUST acknowledge any subsequent PUBLISH packet with the same Packet Identifier by sending a PUBREC
The receiver MUST NOT cause duplicate messages to be delivered to any onward recipients in this case
MUST respond to a PUBREL packet by sending a PUBCOMP packet containing the same Packet Identifier as the PUBREL
the receiver MUST treat any subsequent PUBLISH packet that contains that Packet Identifier as being a new Application Message
MUST continue the QoS 2 acknowledgement sequence even if the receiver has applied message expiry
MUST NOT: MUST NOT re-send the PUBLISH once the sender has sent the corresponding PUBREL packet
MUST NOT apply Message expiry if a PUBLISH packet has been sent
the receiver MUST NOT cause duplicate messages to be delivered to any onward recipients in this case

    "does not require the use of any extensions": "Prohibited"
}

mandatory: will not send Malformed Packets or cause Protocol Errors
prohibited: will not send Malformed Packets or cause Protocol Errors
recommended: SHOULD send a DISCONNECT packet containing the reason code before closing the Network Connection

Mandatory: MUST be sent in WebSocket binary data frames
MUST close the Network Connection
MUST NOT assume that MQTT Control Packets are aligned on WebSocket frame boundaries
MUST include mqtt in the list of WebSocket Sub Protocols the Client offers
MUST be mqtt
Prohibited: MUST NOT assume that MQTT Control Packets are aligned on WebSocket frame boundaries
